title: Black Widower

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation opening the episode
jokes.punchline: The iconic catchphrase itself
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfectly character-integrated and economical, though not original within the show's context

jokes.text: Would you turn off that rock 'n' rock music?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to sound authoritative about music
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'rock 'n' roll' as 'rock 'n' rock'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism - shows his character through linguistic incompetence

jokes.text: Don't have a stegosaurus, man.
jokes.setup: Bart's typical 'Don't have a cow, man' catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Replacing 'cow' with 'stegosaurus' while watching dinosaur show
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that connects Bart's catchphrase to the context - well-crafted variation

jokes.text: These talking dinosaurs are more real than most real families on TV.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on a dinosaur TV show
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's a cartoon character saying this
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about TV families from an animated character - smart self-referential comedy

jokes.text: It's like they saw our lives and put it up on screen.
jokes.setup: Homer relating to the TV dinosaur family
jokes.punchline: The meta-joke that The Simpsons IS a TV family
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the meta-joke - Homer unknowingly describing his own show

jokes.text: Cool, he can teach us how to kill a man with a lunch tray.
jokes.setup: Learning Selma's boyfriend is an ex-convict
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriately enthusiastic response about violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - dark humor delivered with childlike excitement

jokes.text: Then why aren't you using the good silverware? - I'm just not.
jokes.setup: Marge saying the ex-con has paid his debt to society
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out Marge's actions contradict her words, Marge's weak denial
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation comedy - Lisa catches the hypocrisy, Marge's flustered response is perfect

jokes.text: Aah! It's Sideshow Bob!
jokes.setup: Family preparing to meet Selma's mysterious ex-con boyfriend
jokes.punchline: The reveal that it's Bart's nemesis
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure - the audience shares Bart's shock at this twist

jokes.text: Bart is the shamus in short pants who sent me to prison.
jokes.setup: Bob explaining why Bart recognized him
jokes.punchline: The theatrical language 'shamus in short pants' for a kid detective
jokes.explanation: Classic Sideshow Bob - overly theatrical language that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: If some snot-nosed little kid sent me to prison, the first thing out I'd find out where he lives and tear him a new belly-button.
jokes.setup: Patty commenting on what she'd do if imprisoned by a child
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic threat 'tear him a new belly-button'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with creative euphemism - fits Patty's character

jokes.text: You're forgetting the first two noble truths of the Buddha. - I am not. - Yes, you are.
jokes.setup: Bob trying to sound philosophical
jokes.punchline: Selma's immediate contradiction and their petty argument
jokes.explanation: Bob's pretentiousness undercut by Selma's blunt contradiction - good character dynamic

jokes.text: When prison ended that dream, I became a seething cauldron of rage and recrimination.
jokes.setup: Bob explaining his emotional journey
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic language for basic anger
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob verbose pretentiousness - 'seething cauldron' is memorably overwrought

jokes.text: This is one more Emmy than you'll ever win, you bantering jack-in-the-box!
jokes.setup: Bob winning an Emmy and confronting Krusty
jokes.punchline: The insult 'bantering jack-in-the-box' for Krusty
jokes.explanation: Excellent creative insult that perfectly captures both characters - Bob's verbosity vs. Krusty's simple comedy

jokes.text: Just don't drop it in the shower.
jokes.setup: Bob gloating about his Emmy win
jokes.punchline: Krusty's prison rape reference
jokes.explanation: Dark prison humor - economical and cutting comeback from Krusty

jokes.text: Do you know what prison is like for a life-long Republican?
jokes.setup: Bob describing his prison experience
jokes.punchline: The implication that Republicans have it worse in prison
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - establishes Bob's political leanings while commenting on prison demographics

jokes.text: Who used my chap stick? - Oh, I did. Here you go. - I don't want it.
jokes.setup: Bob describing overcrowded prison conditions as 'beasts'
jokes.punchline: The polite chap stick exchange contradicting his description
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - the 'savage' prison conditions are undercut by polite behavior

jokes.text: But what kind of guest would I have been?
jokes.setup: Bob saying if he wanted to kill Bart, he'd have choked him immediately
jokes.punchline: His concern about proper guest etiquette while discussing murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Bob's twisted priorities perfectly expressed through etiquette concerns

jokes.text: No Emmys, no Oscars, not even a Golden Globe.
jokes.setup: Prison guard explaining entertainment awards are contraband
jokes.punchline: The hierarchy ending with Golden Globes as least prestigious
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with insider Hollywood knowledge about award prestige

jokes.text: I share an apartment with my twin sister. Enclosed is a photo of us on a tandem bike. I forget which one I am.
jokes.setup: Selma's letter to the prison pen pal program
jokes.punchline: Can't tell herself apart from her twin sister
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor about identical twins - perfectly captures Selma's practical desperation

jokes.text: your latest letter set off a riot in the maximum-security wing of my heart
jokes.setup: Bob's romantic letter to Selma
jokes.punchline: Prison metaphor applied to emotions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant romantic metaphor using prison imagery - perfectly Bob's theatrical style

jokes.text: Like the lone crocus that pokes through the prison yard, our love bloomed despite all obstacles.
jokes.setup: Bob describing his romance with Selma
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic language about prison romance
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob verbosity - the flower metaphor in prison context is appropriately overwrought

jokes.text: Cutter. Icepick. Snake. I'm going to miss you most of all.
jokes.setup: Bob saying goodbye to cellmates with tough names
jokes.punchline: The 'Wizard of Oz' reference with Dorothy's goodbye to Scarecrow
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference - contrasts tough prison names with sentimental movie moment

jokes.text: You're living proof that our revolving-door prison system works.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Bob's redemption story
jokes.punchline: Calling the flawed 'revolving-door' system a success
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as Marge's naive optimism - excellent irony

jokes.text: You seized the wheel of my slow boat to hell, and pointed it towards the sunny shores of Selma.
jokes.setup: Bob thanking Bart for catching him
jokes.punchline: The elaborate nautical metaphor ending with 'sunny shores of Selma'
jokes.explanation: Classic Bob over-elaboration - the romantic maritime metaphor is absurdly verbose

jokes.text: Though kissing you is like kissing some divine ashtray, that's not what I had in mind.
jokes.setup: Bob about to do something 'bold and shocking'
jokes.punchline: Romantic language comparing Selma to an ashtray
jokes.explanation: Brilliant backhanded compliment - 'divine ashtray' perfectly captures Selma's smoking while maintaining romantic tone

jokes.text: Don't be a fool. That man is scum. - Then call me 'Mrs. Scum.'
jokes.setup: Bart warning Selma about Bob
jokes.punchline: Selma's defiant acceptance of the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selma's desperation trumps good sense, delivered with attitude

jokes.text: 'A'... 'G'... 'Q'... Seven... Close enough. May you drive safely and find true love.
jokes.setup: DMV eye exam for driver's license
jokes.punchline: Accepting completely wrong answers as 'close enough'
jokes.explanation: Great bureaucratic incompetence joke - mixing letters, numbers, and gibberish as acceptable

jokes.text: You should see the bus they came to the studio in.
jokes.setup: TV host calling kids 'tragic' and 'nauseous'
jokes.punchline: The additional insult about their transportation
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited TV host humor - shows media exploitation of children

jokes.text: If they open the books on this telethon I'm right back in there.
jokes.setup: Krusty seemingly forgiving Bob
jokes.punchline: Revealing his own criminal activity with telethon finances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment - his forgiveness is motivated by his own guilt

jokes.text: It looks like ketchup, it tastes like ketchup, but, brother, it ain't ketchup!
jokes.setup: Homer enthusiastically describing cocktail weenie sauce
jokes.punchline: The mystery of what the red sauce actually is
jokes.explanation: Homer's food obsession with mysterious processed food elements - solid character comedy

jokes.text: I bought stock in a Mace company just before society crumbled.
jokes.setup: Selma explaining how she got money
jokes.punchline: Profiting from societal decay through pepper spray investment
jokes.explanation: Dark social commentary - Selma's practical approach to urban decay as investment opportunity

jokes.text: Holy frijoles! We've got ten minutes till MacGyver. Driver, here's a fin. Get me home, and don't spare the whip.
jokes.setup: Selma suddenly realizing the time
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic urgency over a TV show, using old-timey language
jokes.explanation: Selma's obsession with MacGyver shown through urgency and antiquated expressions

jokes.text: Don't thank me. Thank the moon's gravitational pull.
jokes.setup: MacGyver being thanked for saving a village
jokes.punchline: Crediting physics instead of his actions
jokes.explanation: Parody of MacGyver's scientific problem-solving - absurdly crediting astronomy

jokes.text: First of all, he's not a genius. He's an actor. And second, he's not much of an actor.
jokes.setup: Selma calling MacGyver a genius
jokes.punchline: Bob's two-part demolition of the character and actor
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating structure - first destroys the character, then the performance itself

jokes.text: that was a well-plotted piece of non-claptrap that never made me retch.
jokes.setup: Bob trying to say something nice about MacGyver
jokes.punchline: The backhanded compliment using double negatives
jokes.explanation: Bob's theatrical verbosity creates an elaborate non-compliment - perfectly in character

jokes.text: I go to a bar, I pound a few, then I stumble home in the mood for love.
jokes.setup: Homer giving marriage advice about dealing with disagreements
jokes.punchline: His solution is getting drunk to tolerate his wife's shows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - alcohol as marriage counseling, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Bart no like. Bad medicine.
jokes.setup: Bart watching the wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Speaking like a Native American stereotype from old westerns
jokes.explanation: Dated humor relying on stereotype - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, gee, a joke, huh? Um... uh... funny. Okay, this guy walks into a bar and takes out a tiny piano and a 12-inch pianist-- Oh, no, wait. I can't tell that one.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Krusty to say something funny
jokes.punchline: His struggle to be funny on command, starting an inappropriate joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy about comedians' performance pressure - starts a classic dirty joke then stops

jokes.text: 41 years ago, God took 168 pounds of clay, and he made me a woman, and for this, I thank him.
jokes.setup: Selma giving a wedding toast
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific weight and matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Selma's blunt practicality applied to religious sentiment - the specific weight is funny

jokes.text: Lighten up, son. If he was going to commit a crime, would he have invited the number-one cop in town? Now, where did I put my gun? Oh, yeah. I set it down when I got some cake.
jokes.setup: Wiggum dismissing Bart's suspicions about Bob
jokes.punchline: Calling himself 'number-one cop' then immediately losing his gun for cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - the self-aggrandizement immediately undercut by negligence

jokes.text: Enjoy your rest. The wedding was very tough on you, and the honeymoon is going to be... murder.
jokes.setup: Bob seemingly caring for sleeping Selma
jokes.punchline: The word 'murder' as both figure of speech and literal intention
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double meaning - works as innocent comment and sinister threat simultaneously

jokes.text: I wanted a room with a fireplace, you brainless luggage monkey!
jokes.setup: Bob complaining to hotel staff
jokes.punchline: The creative insult 'brainless luggage monkey'
jokes.explanation: Creative Bob insult - 'luggage monkey' is memorably specific to hotel context

jokes.text: Ah, fire-- Scourge of Prometheus, toaster of marshmallows, eradicator of deadwood.
jokes.setup: Bob admiring the hotel fireplace
jokes.punchline: His poetic description mixing mythology with mundane uses
jokes.explanation: Peak Bob verbosity - mixing Greek mythology with camping activities is perfectly pretentious

jokes.text: Even murder has its ugly side.
jokes.setup: Bob dealing with Selma's annoying habits before killing her
jokes.punchline: Treating murder as merely having drawbacks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - understatement about murder's 'inconveniences' is perfectly twisted

jokes.text: Soon I will kill you. - Huh? - Son pieds sentit beau-- French for 'her foot smells lovely.'
jokes.setup: Bob massaging Selma's feet and accidentally speaking his thoughts
jokes.punchline: His quick cover-up with fake French translation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating bit - Bob's thoughts slip out, then increasingly desperate fake translations

jokes.text: Voy a matar a usted. - Wha? - That's Spanish for 'I am going to kill you.' - Say what?
jokes.setup: Bob continuing to accidentally confess
jokes.punchline: Finally telling the truth but claiming it's a translation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation climax - he literally tells her he'll kill her and she doesn't get it

jokes.text: Don't forget to die. - Ding-dong.
jokes.setup: Bob saying goodbye as Selma goes to watch TV
jokes.punchline: His casual murder instruction, Selma's oblivious 'ding-dong' response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect timing - Bob's directness met with Selma's complete obliviousness

jokes.text: My best-laid plans have gang agley.
jokes.setup: Bob's murder plot has failed
jokes.punchline: Quoting Robert Burns in Scottish dialect
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob moment - using obscure poetry reference for his criminal failure

jokes.text: Her only hope was a plucky young boy and his slow-witted father.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining how he solved the case
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact description of Homer as 'slow-witted'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's casual assessment of his father's intelligence

jokes.text: After trying four times to explain it to Homer, I explained it to Mom and we were on our way!
jokes.setup: Bart describing his investigation process
jokes.punchline: Homer's inability to understand after multiple explanations
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer stupidity joke - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Oh, I'll field that one. [EXPLOSION] Oh, right, the gas.
jokes.setup: Someone asking why the room exploded if Selma was saved
jokes.punchline: Homer volunteering to explain, then getting blown up, then understanding
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - Homer's eagerness to help leads to his own explosion

tmdb_overview: Bart suspects something rotten when Selma gets engaged to Sideshow Bob.
insight: Marge's sister episode delivers 51 jokes in 22 minutes—steady pace masks uneven emotional stakes.
